'''Mark Hilton''' (born 31 March 1975), also known by the [[nickname]] of '''"The Beast"''',<ref>{{cite web |title=Hilton finds his numbers finally start to add up |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/hilton-finds-his-numbers-finally-start-to-add-up-560111.html |website=The Independent |access-date=11 January 2024 |date=16 April 2004}}</ref> is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[England national rugby league team|England]], and at club level for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], as a {{rlp|pr}}.

==International honours==
Mark Hilton won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at Warrington in 1995 against France ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]), and in 1999 against France ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]).

==Club career==
Mark Hilton made his début for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] on Saturday 18 December 1993, and he played his last match for Warrington on Saturday 15 July 2006.{{citation needed|date=January 2020}}

==Outside of rugby league==
Hilton retired from rugby league in 2006 to begin a teaching career.<ref>{{cite web |title=It’s all over for Mr Hilton |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/911173.its-all-over-for-mr-hilton/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=11 January 2024 |date=7 September 2006}}</ref> As of 2016, he teaches mathematics at [[Beamont Collegiate Academy]], Warrington, having previously taught at [[Maghull High School]], [[Merseyside]].
